## Purpose
This file serves as the configuration and metadata descriptor for the "diffdoc" project, detailing its dependencies, scripts, and other project-specific settings.

## User-Visible Behavior
Users can install the project and its dependencies using npm, run build scripts to compile TypeScript code, and execute the main application via the command line using the provided binaries.

## Business Rules
The project requires Node.js version 22 or higher. It includes specific scripts for building, cleaning, and starting the application, as well as generating schemas.

## Data Inputs And Outputs
Data inputs include project dependencies and scripts defined in the package.json file. Outputs include compiled JavaScript files in the "dist" directory and generated schemas.

## Side Effects
Running the build or clean scripts will modify the filesystem by creating or deleting files in the "dist" directory.

## Error And Edge Cases
Errors may occur if the required Node.js version is not met, or if there are issues during the execution of scripts, such as missing files or incorrect configurations.

## Dependencies
The project relies on several external libraries for functionality, including SDKs and utilities for command-line interfaces, file handling, and schema validation.

## Operational Notes
The project uses TypeScript for development, and the build process involves compiling TypeScript files and generating schemas. The clean script forcefully removes the "dist" directory, which should be used with caution.
